{{Quote|Vandelhelm is a single planet system, but it has one of the biggest asteroid clouds you'll ever see.|[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]|<ref name="wizards" />}}
The '''Vandelhelm Cloud''' was a vast [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field that encompassed the [[Vandelhelm system/Legends|Vandelhelm system]]<ref name="wizards" /> in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]'s [[Epsi Collective/Legends|Epsi Collective]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The Vandelhelm Cloud was thick enough that the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]], though extremely rich in valuable metals,<ref name="wizards" /> was not discovered until the relatively late date of approximately [[3053 BBY]].<ref name="Vandel">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw200403041vandelhelm|series=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys|text=Part 1: File Vandelhelm-LC/hsnn}} states that [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]] was discovered 22,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Galactic Constitution Conference|founding]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' dates to [[25,053 BBY]]. Thus, Vandelhelm was discovered in approximately [[3053 BBY]].</ref> The cloud's asteroids were so thick and unpredictable that it was reputed to take an ace [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] to navigate it. [[Guild Master/Legends|Guildmaster]] [[Orrk]] of Vandelhelm's [[Metalsmiths Guild]] [[Mine/Legends|mined]] the Vandelhelm Cloud in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] in an attempt to prevent the planet's [[Venerated Ones]] from returning from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]], but [[Alliance of Free Planets]] hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] deftly ran the asteroids in his ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' to return them home.<ref name="SW98">[[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Vandelhelm Cloud briefly appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1985]].<ref name="SW98" /> The cloud was later named in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] supplement ''[[Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys]]'', written by [[Cory J. Herndon]].<ref name="wizards" />
